data.narrative:
Blue Water Autonomy is a Series A defense and security company founded by Rylan Hamilton, Austin Gray, and Scott N. Miller. The company has raised $64M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 publicly traded companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and NODA AI.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses the $64M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $128M. Peer-median analysis anchors to four same-sector, same-stage comparable companies: Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, Ares Industries at $150M, and NODA AI at $125M. The median of this peer set is $225M, with a range spanning $125M to $500M. Triangulating these two methods produces a low estimate of $109M, a median of $176M, and a high of $259M.

The $176M median valuation is supported by both stage-appropriate growth expectations and peer benchmarking, though the wide peer dispersion ($125M–$500M) reflects material uncertainty in comparability. Key sensitivities include the extent to which Blue Water's technology differentiation, defensibility, and customer traction align with higher-quartile peers versus median-stage entrants. The analysis supports a range of $109M–$259M, with a central case near $176M.
